name: cloudformation
context: Provision and manage AWS infrastructure using CloudFormation templates
info: AWS CloudFormation lets you define cloud infrastructure as code using JSON or YAML templates. A template describes a collection of AWS resources (EC2 instances, S3 buckets, Lambda functions, IAM roles, RDS databases, etc.) as a stack. CloudFormation provisions and configures resources in the correct order, handles dependencies, and supports rollback on failure. Change sets let you preview modifications before applying them. Nested stacks compose large infrastructures from reusable templates. The AWS CDK (Cloud Development Kit) generates CloudFormation templates from TypeScript, Python, Java, or Go code. The AWS SDK v3 `@aws-sdk/client-cloudformation` package provides CreateStack, UpdateStack, DeleteStack, and DescribeStacks commands.
body:
# AWS CloudFormation

CloudFormation is AWS's infrastructure-as-code service. Define your entire infrastructure in a JSON or YAML template, and CloudFormation provisions and manages it as a stack. Stacks are atomic — if any resource fails to create, the entire stack rolls back.

## Template Anatomy

A CloudFormation template has these sections:

```yaml
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: My application stack

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Type: String
    AllowedValues: [dev, staging, prod]
    Default: dev

Resources:
  MyBucket:
    Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
    Properties:
      BucketName: !Sub 'my-app-${Environment}'
      VersioningConfiguration:
        Status: Enabled

  MyFunction:
    Type: AWS::Lambda::Function
    Properties:
      FunctionName: !Sub 'my-function-${Environment}'
      Runtime: nodejs20.x
      Handler: index.handler
      Role: !GetAtt MyRole.Arn
      Code:
        S3Bucket: !Ref MyBucket
        S3Key: function.zip

  MyRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: lambda.amazonaws.com
            Action: sts:AssumeRole

Outputs:
  BucketArn:
    Value: !GetAtt MyBucket.Arn
  FunctionArn:
    Value: !GetAtt MyFunction.Arn
```

Key sections: Parameters (input values), Resources (AWS resources to create), Outputs (values to export). Use `!Ref` and `!GetAtt` to reference resources and their attributes.

## Deploy a Stack

```javascript
import { CloudFormationClient, CreateStackCommand } from "@aws-sdk/client-cloudformation";
import { readFileSync } from "fs";

const cfn = new CloudFormationClient({ region: process.env.AWS_REGION });

await cfn.send(new CreateStackCommand({
  StackName: "my-app-stack",
  TemplateBody: readFileSync("./template.yaml", "utf-8"),
  Parameters: [
    { ParameterKey: "Environment", ParameterValue: "prod" },
  ],
  Capabilities: ["CAPABILITY_IAM"], // required when creating IAM resources
}));
```

Include `CAPABILITY_IAM` or `CAPABILITY_NAMED_IAM` when your template creates [[aws/iam]] resources.

## Parameters and Outputs

Parameters allow templates to be reusable across environments (dev, staging, prod). Define type, constraints, and defaults. Outputs export values (ARNs, endpoints, IDs) that other stacks or scripts can consume. Use `Fn::ImportValue` to reference outputs across stacks.

## Change Sets

Before updating a stack, create a change set to preview what will change:

1. `CreateChangeSet` — generate a diff of proposed changes
2. Review additions, modifications, and deletions
3. `ExecuteChangeSet` — apply the changes
4. Or `DeleteChangeSet` — discard without applying

Change sets prevent unexpected resource replacements or deletions.

## Nested Stacks

Break large templates into reusable nested stacks:

```yaml
Resources:
  NetworkStack:
    Type: AWS::CloudFormation::Stack
    Properties:
      TemplateURL: https://s3.amazonaws.com/my-templates/network.yaml
      Parameters:
        VpcCidr: '10.0.0.0/16'
```

Nested stacks promote reuse and keep individual templates manageable. Store nested templates in [[aws/s3]].

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | IAM access key for the CloudFormation client |
| `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | IAM secret key for the CloudFormation client |
| `AWS_REGION` | Target region for stack deployment |

sections.title: Template anatomy
sections.body: Templates have Parameters (inputs), Resources (AWS resources), and Outputs (exported values). Resources use types like `AWS::S3::Bucket`, `AWS::Lambda::Function`, `AWS::IAM::Role` — corresponding to [[aws/s3]], [[aws/lambda]], [[aws/iam]] services. Use `!Ref` for resource references and `!GetAtt` for attributes. `!Sub` for string substitution. DependsOn controls creation order.

sections.title: Parameters and outputs
sections.body: Parameters make templates reusable: define Environment (dev/staging/prod), instance types, bucket names as inputs. Use AllowedValues, Default, and constraints. Outputs export stack values (ARNs, endpoints, URLs) for cross-stack references via `Fn::ImportValue`. Use SSM Parameter Store for shared configuration across stacks.

sections.title: Change sets
sections.body: Change sets preview updates before applying them. Create with `CreateChangeSetCommand`, review the diff (additions, modifications, replacements, deletions), then execute or discard. Critical for production stacks where unexpected resource replacement could cause downtime. Always use change sets for updates to stacks containing [[aws/rds]] databases or [[aws/ec2]] instances.

sections.title: Nested stacks
sections.body: Use `AWS::CloudFormation::Stack` to embed child stacks. Store nested templates in [[aws/s3]]. Pass parameters down and receive outputs up. Common patterns: network stack (VPC, subnets), compute stack ([[aws/ec2]], [[aws/lambda]]), data stack ([[aws/s3]], [[aws/rds]]). Keeps individual templates under the 1 MB limit.

scripts.description: Create or update an AWS CloudFormation stack from a template

depends.reason: IAM credentials with cloudformation:CreateStack permission are required
